title: Startup says sound waves can replace fire sprinklers; experts aren't so sure
description: Acoustic fire suppression goes commercial.

description: Plus: The NSA tests Anthropic’s Mythos Preview to find vulnerabilities, a Finnish teen is charged over the Scattered Spider hacking spree, and more.

title: Study: AI models that consider user's feeling are more likely to make errors
description: Overtuning can cause models to "prioritize user satisfaction over truthfulness.”

title: Meta buys robotics startup to bolster its humanoid AI ambitions
description: Meta bought humanoid startup Assured Robot Intelligence to beef up its AI models for robots, the company said.

title: Musk v. Altman week 1: Elon Musk says he was duped, warns AI could kill us all, and admits that xAI distills OpenAI’s models
description: In the first week of the landmark trial between Elon Musk and OpenAI, Musk took the stand in a crisp black suit and tie and argued that OpenAI CEO Sam Altman and president Greg Brockman had deceived him into bankrolling the company. title: The RAMpocalypse has bought Microsoft valuable time in the fight against SteamOS
description: Op-ed: Valve has made a dent in Windows' gaming share, but can it keep going?

title: Man dies covered in necrotic lesions after amoebas eat him alive
description: Doctors suspect three factors, each unremarkable on its own, contributed to his fate.

title: A Dark-Money Campaign Is Paying Influencers to Frame Chinese AI as a Threat
description: Build American AI, a nonprofit linked to a super PAC bankrolled by executives at OpenAI and Andreessen Horowitz, is funding a campaign to spread pro-AI messaging and stoke fears about China.

title: Ubuntu infrastructure has been down for more than a day
description: The outage has hampered communication concerning a critical vulnerability that gives root.

title: Did you know you can’t steal a charity? Don’t worry. Elon Musk will remind you.
description: Elon Musk spent the better part of three days on the witness stand this week in his lawsuit against OpenAI, and it’s already getting messy. title: Pentagon inks deals with Nvidia, Microsoft, and AWS to deploy AI on classified networks
description: The deals come as the DOD has doubled down on diversifying its exposure to AI vendors in the wake of its controversial dispute with Anthropic over usage terms of its AI models.

title: Cyber-Insecurity in the AI Era
description: Cybersecurity was already under strain before AI entered the stack. title: Operationalizing AI for Scale and Sovereignty
description: Companies are taking control of their own data to tailor AI for their needs. The challenge lies in balancing ownership with the safe, trusted flow of high‑quality data needed to power reliable insights. title: The Download: a new Christian phone network, and debugging LLMs
description: This is today’s edition of The Download, our weekday newsletter that provides a daily dose of what’s going on in the world of technology. title: Inexpensive seafloor-hopping submersibles could stoke deep-sea science—and mining
description: Smack dab between Australia and South America, the US National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration (NOAA) research vessel Rainier is currently on a mission to map more than 8,000 square nautical miles of the Pacific seafloor in search of critical mineral deposits. title: How Shivon Zilis Operated as Elon Musk’s OpenAI Insider
description: Messages presented at trial reveal how Zilis, the mother of four of Musk’s children, acted as an intermediary between him and OpenAI.

title: Good Luck Getting a Mac Mini for the Next ‘Several Months’
description: Apple CEO Tim Cook told analysts that AI adoption has happened faster than expected.

title: Musk v. Altman Kicks Off, DOJ Guts Voting Rights Unit, and Is the AI Job Apocalypse Overhyped?
description: In this episode of Uncanny Valley, we get into how the Elon Musk-Sam Altman trial goes way beyond their rivalry and could have major implications both for OpenAI and also the AI industry at large.

title: Understanding Convolutions on Graphs
description: Understanding the building blocks and design choices of graph neural networks.

title: A Gentle Introduction to Graph Neural Networks
description: What components are needed for building learning algorithms that leverage the structure and properties of graphs?

title: Distill Hiatus
description: After five years, Distill will be taking a break.

title: Adversarial Reprogramming of Neural Cellular Automata
description: Reprogramming Neural CA to exhibit novel behaviour, using adversarial attacks.

title: Weight Banding
description: Weights in the final layer of common visual models appear as horizontal bands. We investigate how and why.
